mnemonics: CPU CHEW: Control Unit, Processing (ALU), and Highway (Buses) for External Work
keyPoints: A CPU is composed of three main functional units: The Control Unit (CU), the Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU), and the system Buses (Address, Data, Control).
The Control Unit (CU) is the 'manager' that directs the operation of the processor by interpreting instructions and sending control signals to other components.
Registers are small, high-speed storage locations inside the CPU used to hold data, instructions, and addresses currently being processed.
commonMistakes: Confusing the roles of the ALU and Control Unit (CU); the ALU performs calculations, the CU directs traffic.
Forgetting that the Memory Address Register (MAR) holds a location, while the Memory Data Register (MDR) holds the actual value from that location.
Assuming data bus width determines maximum memory size; it's the address bus width (2^(n) bytes) that sets this limit.
